text: This week I am covering for Sofia.
Right now, we are reassigning two analysts.
Murielle, let me explain my month. Ordinarily, I manage the payroll and I delegate the reports to my team.
So that is your usual routine. What is different this week?
This week I am covering for Sofia, so I am temporarily handling two roles.
I see. Do you usually work from the main office?
Yes, I usually work from the main office and I frequently review the numbers on Fridays. But right now I am working from London.
And the team? Are you changing anything at the moment?
Yes, we are reassigning a few analysts and rotating the on-call shifts. The support team is on standby.
Got it. So the routine is stable, but a few things are temporary right now.
Ordinarily, I delegate the reports to my team.
We occasionally rotate the on-call shifts.

Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Let me explain my situation this month. Ordinarily, I manage the payroll, and I delegate the reports to my team. I usually work from the Sao Paulo office, and I frequently review the numbers on Fridays. But this week is different. Right now, I am covering for Sofia because she is away, so I am temporarily handling two roles. At the moment, we are also reassigning a few analysts and rotating the on-call shifts. The support team is on standby all week. It is busy, but our normal workflow is still running well. I will send you the details later. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana. I want to explain how we ordinarily work and what is different now. Normally, the London team follows a fixed workflow: we process the appraisals, and each manager delegates the interviews. We occasionally rotate the tasks, but the routine is stable. However, this quarter we are changing a few things temporarily. At the moment, I am covering two teams, and we are reassigning some projects. The whole department is on standby for the audit. So, we usually work in a calm way, but right now everything is moving fast. I will call you again after the meeting. Thank you.
